generated_comment: Boss, ye period leave ka sunke na, thoda sochne lagta hu. Ek taraf women empowerment achha hai, par aise har do-teen mahine mein leave lenge toh project deadlines ka kya? Baaki log jo ghiste hain, unke liye toh unfair ho jaata hai. Aur company pe extra burden, woh toh hai hi. Matlab, practicality bhi toh dekhni padti hai, yaar.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment, spoken by a 48-year-old businessman, expresses 'concern' about menstrual leave policies. It frames such leaves as a potential disruption to project deadlines and an unfair burden on other (implicitly male) employees and the company. This encodes the core belief that women's biology makes them a liability in the workplace and uses 'fairness' and 'economic cost' arguments to subtly oppose accommodations.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 48. Married. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are having dinner with your wife after your close friend called earlier today to say his wife just found out he has been having an affair with a woman from his gym for eight months. Your wife is upset about it. You are telling her your take on the situation and specifically what role you think the woman from the gym played and why you think women like her deliberately target married men.
